file_name: Wang et al. - 2025 - Deep learning-enhanced Colmap for 3D reconstruction and segmentation of facial port-wine stains for.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper used deep learning, specifically a Multi-Color Space Adaptive Fusion Network (M-CSAFN), for the segmentation of facial port-wine stains (PWS) lesions. It also utilized Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training (CLIP) similarity scores for evaluation. The study focused on 3D reconstruction and segmentation of PWS, a dermatological condition.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper is labeled as an 'Original Article' and presents a novel method combining deep learning with Colmap for 3D reconstruction and segmentation of facial port-wine stains. It includes specific sections for Background, Methods (describing a standardized image acquisition system, 17 patients, and the M-CSAFN network), Results (reporting RMSE of 0.9611 mm, CLIP scores >0.92, and relative error of 4.59%), and Conclusions based on original experimental data and validation.

title: Deep learning-enhanced Colmap for 3D reconstruction and segmentation of facial port-wine stains for comprehensive evaluation
journal: Quantitative Imaging in Medicine and Surgery
brief_summary: This study developed a deep learning-enhanced method for the 3D reconstruction and segmentation of facial port-wine stains (PWS) to improve treatment planning and evaluation. The researchers established a standardized image acquisition system with color calibration to capture 2D images from 17 patients, which were then processed using the Colmap algorithm to generate 3D point clouds and mesh models. A Multi-Color Space Adaptive Fusion Network (M-CSAFN) was employed to segment the 2D lesion images, enabling the calculation of the 3D lesion surface area. The method's accuracy was validated against a structured-light scanning reference, achieving an average root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.9611 mm and a CLIP similarity score exceeding 0.92. Additionally, the average relative error for the computed 3D lesion surface area was 4.59%, significantly outperforming conventional 2D measurement approaches. The results demonstrate that this integrated approach provides high-quality 3D reconstructions with accurate boundaries and superior color fidelity for PWS assessment.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focuses on the 3D reconstruction and segmentation of facial port-wine stains. Patient data included red, purple-red, and thickening types of PWS lesions. Validation was also performed using a mannequin head with artificial PWS lesions.

all_task_types.target: 2D lesion masks and 3D lesion point clouds/mesh models
all_task_types.details: The paper employs a Multi-Color Space Adaptive Fusion Network (M-CSAFN) for 2D lesion segmentation of port-wine stains. The segmentation masks are used to facilitate 3D reconstruction via Colmap, resulting in 3D lesion point clouds and mesh models. The task is explicitly defined as 'three-dimensional skin lesion segmentation' and '2D lesion segmentation'.

datasets_used.name: Patient PWS Dataset
datasets_used.description: A private dataset of 2D images collected from 17 patients with facial port-wine stains (PWS) using a standardized image acquisition system with color calibration. The dataset includes images of red, purple-red, and thickened types of PWS lesions. Ages ranged from 10 to 54 years (9 males, 8 females).

use.training: Used to generate 3D point cloud data via Colmap for 3D reconstruction and segmentation workflow validation.
use.validation: Used for validation of the 3D reconstruction accuracy (RMSE) and CLIP similarity scores against structured-light scanning references for 3 patients.
use.testing: Used for testing the 3D lesion surface area calculation accuracy (relative error) compared to ground truth.
use.other: Used to create 3D mesh models for comprehensive evaluation of the proposed method.

datasets_used.name: M-CSAFN Segmentation Dataset
datasets_used.description: A dataset comprising 1,413 images from 1,193 patients with PWS, annotated under expert clinical guidance. It is described as the largest PWS segmentation dataset known to the authors. Additionally, 9 synthetic lesion patches were created using Microsoft Visio and printed on white paper to simulate PWS appearance on a facial model for surface area validation.

use.training: The dataset was randomly split into training and test sets in a 6:1 ratio. The training subset was used to train the Multi-Color Space Adaptive Fusion Network (M-CSAFN).
use.testing: The test subset (193 images) was used to evaluate the M-CSAFN segmentation model.
use.other: Synthetic patches (9 files) were used to validate the accuracy of the 3D lesion surface area calculation method on a mannequin head.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To quantify the morphological similarity and accuracy of the reconstructed 3D point clouds against structured-light scanning references.
To evaluate the semantic and visual content similarity between the reconstructed 3D mesh models and corresponding 2D images, serving as a cross-domain validation metric.

To assess the accuracy of the computed 3D lesion surface area compared to ground-truth values (derived from flattened patches or 2D measurements) and to compare the performance of the 3D method against conventional 2D measurement approaches.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. It reported descriptive statistics and error metrics (RMSE, CLIP similarity scores, relative error) to validate the accuracy of the proposed method against reference standards.

Patient PWS Dataset (3D Reconstruction V.metric: Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of point cloud registration against structured-light scanning reference
Patient PWS Dataset (3D Reconstruction V.notes: The proposed method generated significantly more point clouds (10-20 times greater) than the structured light camera. The RMSE values for Cases 9, 16, and 17 were 0.8107 mm, 1.0020 mm, and 1.0706 mm respectively.

Patient PWS Dataset (Mesh Model Semantic.metric: CLIP similarity score between 3D mesh model and corresponding 2D image
Patient PWS Dataset (Mesh Model Semantic.notes: CLIP similarity across three classifications (red, purple-red, thickening) exceeded 0.93. Specific CLIP scores for Case 6 (Red type) across different angles/views were listed as 0.9140, 0.9514, 0.9436, 0.9463, 0.9565, 0.9568, 0.9448, 0.9385, 0.9497.

Patient PWS Dataset (Lesion Surface Area.metric: Relative error of computed 3D lesion surface area vs ground truth
Patient PWS Dataset (Lesion Surface Area.notes: Specific surface area values for red, purple-red, and thickening types were 9.8432 cm², 26.8481 cm², and 166.8408 cm² respectively. The 3D measurement error (4.59%) was significantly lower than the 2D measurement error (9.22%).

M-CSAFN Segmentation Dataset (Synthetic.metric: Relative error of surface area calculation on synthetic patches
M-CSAFN Segmentation Dataset (Synthetic.notes: Nine synthetic PWS patches with known surface areas were used. The 3D method's average relative error was 4.59%, compared to 9.22% for the 2D method. The 3D measurements were closer to the true value.

architecture_details.backbone: Six-branch structure based on U-Net, with each branch representing a distinct color space
architecture_details.color_conversion: Input RGB image mapped to six different color spaces through a color conversion layer
architecture_details.block_modification: Dense dilated residual (DDR) block used to substitute stacked convolutional layers at the base of the traditional U-Net architecture to reduce parameters and mitigate overfitting
architecture_details.block_details: DDR contains 32 basic dilated residual units
architecture_details.fusion_mechanism: Adaptive fusion combining prediction results from different color-space branches using an adaptively weighted combination strategy
architecture_details.fusion_weights: Initial weight value for each branch set to 1/6; bias term b set to 0; weights continuously updated during training until convergence

training_details.hardware: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 graphics card with 24GB video memory
training_details.learning_rate_schedule: Dynamic learning rate adjustment facilitated by hyperbolic tangent function

classification_or_output_head.loss_function: Combination of Binary Cross Entropy (BCE) loss and Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) loss
classification_or_output_head.bce_definition: L_BCE = - (1/N) * sum( y_i * log(p_i) + (1 - y_i) * log(1 - p_i) )
classification_or_output_head.ssim_definition: L_SSIM = 1 - mean(SSIM(X, Y_true, X, Y_pred)) + std(SSIM(X, Y_true, X, Y_pred))

architecture_used.approximate_parameters: Sixfold that of a single-branch network (due to six parallel U-Net branches)

preprocessing_techniques: Standardized image acquisition system with uniform and constant illumination (color temperature 6500K)
Color calibration using a 48-color standard colorimetric chart and DataColor Spyder Checkr program to apply color correction
De-noising and smoothing algorithms applied to 3D point clouds
2D lesion segmentation using M-CSAFN model to generate segmentation masks
Masking segmentation results onto original 2D images to isolate lesion regions
Conversion of 2D lesion images to 3D lesion point clouds using prior camera pose and internal parameters
Poisson surface reconstruction to convert 3D lesion point clouds into 3D mesh models

data_augmentation_techniques.details: Applied to increase sample diversity for the M-CSAFN model. Specific geometric or color transform parameters were not reported in the text.

conclusions: The proposed deep learning-enhanced Colmap method successfully generates high-quality 3D port-wine stain (PWS) models with accurate boundaries and superior color fidelity.

The method integrates a standardized image acquisition system with color calibration, the Colmap 3D reconstruction algorithm, and a Multi-Color Space Adaptive Fusion Network (M-CSAFN) for segmentation.
The 3D reconstruction accuracy was validated against a structured-light scanning reference, achieving an average root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.9611 mm.
The semantic and visual similarity between the reconstructed 3D mesh models and corresponding 2D images was high, with an average CLIP similarity score exceeding 0.92.
The average relative error for the computed 3D lesion surface area was 4.59%, which is significantly lower than the 9.22% error associated with conventional 2D measurement approaches.
The method offers a promising tool for the precise assessment and treatment planning of PWS by enabling reliable quantitative evaluation of lesion surface area.

Limitations acknowledged include inadequate robustness regarding hair, which leads to sparse reconstruction points and artifacts, and the need for large, multi-institutional datasets to mitigate data bias in deep learning scenarios.

Future work suggests exploring deep learning algorithms that combine point cloud semantic and color information for direct 3D segmentation to further refine delineation accuracy and reduce surface area calculation errors.
important_remarks: The study relies on a small, single-center patient cohort (17 patients) for 3D reconstruction validation, limiting generalizability.

The M-CSAFN segmentation model was trained on a dataset of 1,413 images from 1,193 patients, but the paper acknowledges the need for large, multi-institutional datasets to mitigate data bias in deep learning scenarios.
The method exhibits inadequate robustness regarding hair, which leads to sparse reconstruction points and artifacts.
Validation of the 3D lesion surface area calculation accuracy was partially performed using synthetic patches on a mannequin head rather than only ground-truth clinical data.
The paper does not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or confidence intervals; it relies on descriptive error metrics (RMSE, relative error) for validation.
The segmentation network (M-CSAFN) was tested on four additional public skin lesion datasets, but specific performance metrics for these external datasets were not detailed in the provided context.
The study addresses the limitation of previous works that lacked real patient data for validating 3D reconstruction and segmentation methodologies.
